title: cystopteris fragilis subsp. dickieana
intro: Cystopteris fragilis subsp. dickieana is a rare and endemic fern species found primarily in Ireland, classified as a subspecies of the fragile fern (Cystopteris fragilis). It is used in traditional herbal medicine for its purported medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which are believed to aid in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Cystopteris fragilis subsp. dickieana are thought to include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and exhibiting antimicrobial activity. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of infusions or tinctures, and its dried fronds are sometimes incorporated into salves and creams to harness its medicinal properties.
